key_departures.impact: Four-time Pro Bowler and 2024 sack leader (17.5) departs after the Bengals declined to franchise tag him. Missed most of 2025 due to core muscle surgery. Had prolonged contract dispute with Cincinnati. Despite injury concerns and market gap (~$10M/yr between his ask and offers), his departure removes the most productive pass rusher on the roster. Bengals signed Boye Mafe as a partial replacement but the production gap is significant.

Signed 3yr/$36M with Jets. Rotational edge rusher who accumulated 14.5 sacks in his Bengals career. Departure removes rotational EDGE depth and further thins the pass rush room. With both Hendrickson and Ossai gone, the Bengals lost their entire EDGE pass rush corps from 2025.
Rotational edge rusher. Departure further depletes an already thin pass rush room, though his role was modest.
Signed a two-year deal with the Saints. Was the Bengals' starting TE in 2025. His departure creates a need at TE with no clear in-house replacement at the starter level.
Signed 1yr/$4.215M with Titans. Was a reserve/depth guard for Cincinnati. Departure is a minor IOL depth loss; Risner's re-signing offsets much of this.
Reserve safety who never met expectations with the Bengals. Departure has minimal impact on the need picture; team was already seeking a starting safety upgrade.
Declined CB who was unlikely to return. Departure has minimal impact as he was not a meaningful starter.
Veteran backup IOL depth piece. Low-impact departure; Risner re-signing and existing OL depth absorb this.
Veteran backup QB. Departure creates a minor need for a backup QB; Jake Browning (RFA) is expected to return but competition may be needed.
Depth linebacker. Departure minimally affects the LB need picture.
Depth CB. Departure has minimal impact.
Depth safety. Departure has minimal need impact; S was already a top need before free agency.

signings.analysis: Mafe is a 3-year, $20M AAV deal — mid-to-upper starter tier for EDGE. He posted a top-8 pass rush win rate in 2025 despite just 2 sacks, was a Super Bowl contributor for Seattle, and has shown a peak of 9 sacks in 2023. The structure is a real investment — 3 years with Bengals-typical heavy Year 1 cash (~$19M guaranteed via bonus) — but guaranteed money is notably below elite EDGE market. He is a clear upgrade over depth alternatives but is not a Hendrickson-tier replacement. With both Hendrickson AND Ossai departed, Mafe partially addresses the need. EDGE is reduced from critical to moderate: the Bengals still need another EDGE via draft or free agency (especially with Shemar Stewart still developing from 2025 rookie year).
signings.sources_note: Confirmed by agent Mike McCartney and Adam Schefter on March 9, 2026. Spotrac confirms 3yr/$60M, AAV $20M. Guaranteed per Kelsey Conway/Cincinnati Enquirer reporting ~$19M signing bonus structure.

signings.analysis: Cook is the top signing of the Bengals' offseason by impact. Ranked 4th overall and 6th in coverage grade among safeties on PFF in 2025. The 3yr/$40.25M deal at $13.4M AAV is mid-tier safety starter money (market tier: $13-15M/yr is solidly starter range). He joins Jordan Battle to form a legitimate starting safety tandem. Pay structure is characteristically Bengals: $18M in Year 1 cash vs. only $14M fully guaranteed, but three years of commitment is a real investment for this organization. S moves from critical need to addressed. Still plausible to add draft depth, but starter need is solved.
signings.sources_note: Confirmed by Tom Pelissero (NFL Network/The Insiders) on March 9, 2026. OverTheCap and Spotrac confirm 3yr/$40.25M ($14M guaranteed, $13.4M AAV). Bengals.com tracker confirmed.

signings.analysis: Risner re-signed to a 1-year, $3.25M deal (up to $5M with incentives) before the legal tampering period opened. He was a starter-quality guard in 2025 with a career-high 69.4 PFF grade and 98.0% pass block efficiency. His retention keeps the entire 2025 starting OL intact (Brown Jr., Fairchild, Karras, Risner, Mims). Despite the one-year bridge structure (which signals the team isn't committing long-term), the starting OL continuity is a major stabilizing factor for Joe Burrow's protection. IOL remains a long-term need (one-year deals at RG and C for Karras) but is not an immediate critical need in 2026.
signings.sources_note: Official team announcement March 2, 2026. Contract details per ESPN/Adam Schefter and Drew Rosenhaus: 1yr base $3.25M, up to $5M. Spotrac confirms $3.25M base value.

signings.analysis: Davis re-signed for 1yr/$1.4M as the nickel/slot CB. He played a breakout volume of snaps in 2025 after spending years as a rotational piece. Minimum-tier deal signals clear depth/special teams role. Does not address any CB need at starter level; provides roster continuity at slot corner at minimum cost.
signings.sources_note: Official team announcement Feb. 20, 2026. Spotrac confirms 1yr/$1.4M ($100K guaranteed).

signings.analysis: Practice squad re-sign. Has appeared in only 3 career games with 2 carries. Essentially a camp body/PS player. No needs impact.
signings.sources_note: Official team announcement Feb. 20, 2026. Contract value estimated at rookie minimum; exact figures not publicly confirmed.

signings.analysis: Depth WR/special teams re-sign. Does not affect any WR need given the team already has Chase and Higgins as a top receiving duo.
signings.sources_note: Official team announcement Feb. 18, 2026. Contract value estimated at veteran minimum.

signings.analysis: Backup TE on a one-year extension. Does not replace the production of departed starter Noah Fant. TE remains a meaningful need despite Hudson's retention — the team needs a starter-quality option.
signings.sources_note: Official team announcement Jan. 19, 2026. Contract value estimated at veteran minimum.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Bryan Cook signing (3yr/$40.25M, $14M guaranteed) fully addresses the starting safety need. Cook was PFF's 4th-ranked safety overall and 6th in coverage grade in 2025. He pairs with Jordan Battle to form a legitimate starting tandem. Safety is no longer a premium draft need — a day-3 developmental pick is fine but not required.

Boye Mafe (3yr/$60M) is a meaningful starter-tier signing but does not fully replace Trey Hendrickson (4x Pro Bowler, 17.5 sacks in 2024) or Joseph Ossai (rotational). The Bengals lost their entire EDGE room and replaced it with one player. Mafe is a legitimate starter, but the depth is gone and Shemar Stewart (2025 1st-round pick) is still developing. EDGE should remain a tier-2 priority in the draft — the Bengals need a complement to Mafe and cannot rely solely on Stewart.

Dalton Risner's re-sign (1yr/$3.25M) keeps the entire 2025 starting OL intact. However, both Risner (RG) and Ted Karras (C) are on 1-year deals, meaning IOL is a significant 2027 concern. For 2026, the line is stable and the need drops slightly. A depth/developmental IOL pick in rounds 3-5 is still smart given the expiring contracts up front.

Noah Fant's departure to the Saints leaves a genuine starter void at TE. Only Tanner Hudson (minimum deal, backup-caliber) remains. The Bengals need a starting-quality TE to support Joe Burrow's offense. This is now a tier-2 draft or free agency need — rounds 2-4 in the draft or a second-wave free agent signing.

No DT/IDL signings in the first wave of free agency. The need is unchanged. T.J. Slaton remains a potential cut candidate. Cincinnati still needs an interior pass rusher to complement their defensive scheme under Al Golden. Active draft need through rounds 2-4.

No LB signing confirmed through March 11. The Bengals had flagged LB as a need at the combine; Demetrius Knight Jr. and Barrett Carter are developmental options but the team needs a proven starter. Still a tier-2 need — second wave of free agency or draft rounds 2-3.

Jalen Davis re-signed on a minimum deal as the slot/nickel. CB Taylor-Britt and Marco Wilson both departed, further thinning the depth. DJ Turner II is the primary starter but the CB2 role remains unsettled. Need stays at tier 3 — a mid-round draft pick or second-wave FA at CB is warranted.

Joe Flacco departed and is unsigned. Jake Browning (RFA) is expected to return under a qualifying offer but his status is not yet confirmed. The Bengals need at least one legitimate backup QB behind Burrow. A second-wave minimum or prove-it deal for a veteran backup is likely. Modest but real need created by Flacco's departure.

key_additions.headline: Super Bowl champion pass rusher (top-8 in pass rush win rate in 2025) replaces departed Hendrickson as the Bengals' new EDGE anchor
PFF's 4th-ranked safety in 2025 fills Cincinnati's biggest secondary need alongside Jordan Battle
Starter-quality guard re-signs on a team-friendly deal, preserving the Bengals' entire 2025 offensive line
Veteran nickel back returns on a minimum deal for slot corner continuity

key_losses.headline: Four-time Pro Bowler and 2024 sack leader (17.5) departs after Bengals decline franchise tag, ending a contentious two-year contract standoff
Rotational pass rusher leaves for New York, completing Cincinnati's loss of their entire 2025 EDGE corps
Starting tight end departs, creating a real void in the Bengals' red zone offense
Reserve guard departs but impact is softened by Risner's re-signing

notes: Free agency officially began March 11 at 4pm ET. Deals agreed to during the legal tampering window (March 9-11) are included as confirmed per NFL.com, ESPN, Spotrac, and OverTheCap. Trey Hendrickson remains unsigned as of the updated_through date — linked to Ravens and Colts but no deal confirmed. Guaranteed money for Mafe ($19M) based on Kelsey Conway/Cincinnati Enquirer reporting on bonus structure; Spotrac lists $60M total/3yr confirmed. Bryan Cook guaranteed ($14M) confirmed via OverTheCap and Tom Pelissero. Cap space post-FA estimated at $25M based on Mike Petraglia's reported $25-30M remaining figure after Cook and Mafe signings.
